ddls:
CREATE TABLE "jvm_instances" (
  "jvm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each JVM instance */,
  "application_name" TEXT /* Name of the application running on the JVM */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server where the JVM is hosted */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the JVM was started */,
  "jvm_version" TEXT /* Version of the JVM being used */,
  "jvm_args" TEXT /* Command-line arguments passed to the JVM */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the JVM is running (e.g., production, staging, development) */,
  "last_heartbeat" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last heartbeat from the JVM */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("jvm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "memory_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory metric entry */,
  "jvm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the JVM instance it pertains to */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., Max, Available, Total, Free, Used) */,
  "memory_value" REAL /* Memory value in Megabytes */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the metric was recorded */,
  "metric_source" TEXT /* Source of the metric (e.g., JVM, external monitoring tool) */,
  "gc_count" INTEGER /* Number of garbage collection events at the time the metric was recorded */,
  "gc_time" INTEGER /* Time spent on garbage collection at the time the metric was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_metrics_jvm_id FOREIGN KEY ("jvm_id") REFERENCES jvm_instances ("jvm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "memory_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory status entry */,
  "jvm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the JVM instance */,
  "phase" TEXT /* Phase of JVM lifecycle (e.g., After Startup, After Initialization, Now) */,
  "memory_snapshot" TEXT /* Snapshot of memory metrics in a formatted string */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the snapshot was taken */,
  "gc_count" INTEGER /* Number of garbage collection events at the time the snapshot was taken */,
  "gc_time" INTEGER /* Time spent on garbage collection at the time the snapshot was taken */,
  "heap_memory" TEXT /* Heap memory usage details */,
  "non_heap_memory" TEXT /* Non-heap memory usage details */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_status_jvm_id FOREIGN KEY ("jvm_id") REFERENCES jvm_instances ("jvm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "jvm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the JVM instance related to the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., Memory Usage High) */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Threshold value that triggered the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was triggered */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the alert has been resolved */,
  "alert_priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the alert */,
  "resolution_steps" TEXT /* Steps taken to resolve the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_jvm_id FOREIGN KEY ("jvm_id") REFERENCES jvm_instances ("jvm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_actions" (
  "action_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each action logged */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who performed the action */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., View Alert, Acknowledge Alert) */,
  "action_description" TEXT /* Description of the action */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action was performed */,
  "jvm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the JVM instance related to the action (if applicable) */,
  "application_name" TEXT /* Name of the application related to the action (if applicable) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client performing the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("action_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_actions_jvm_id FOREIGN KEY ("jvm_id") REFERENCES jvm_instances ("jvm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_actions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
